raw_consumer_statement: In XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I became involved in an employment scam with a company out of XXXX XXXX called XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. As a result of this 'employment ', XXXX XXXX XXXX sent me Bank account information ( bank name, routing number, and account number ) and indicated that they had added me as a cosignee. I was directed to use the automated payment system for my personal credit cards ( XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX and Citibank ) and transfer money from the account to my credit cards. I was then directed to make specific purchases of an equivalent amount at a number of different retail locations, take the product to a XXXX XXXXXXXX store, where it was shipped to XXXX XXXX. After about XXXX XXXX of this type of activity, I told XXXX XXXX XXXX that I would no longer post any more payments to my credit card as they had nor fulfilled the terms of the employment agreement. Shortly thereafter, I began to receive chargebacks of every single transfer, the oldest of which was 60 days old. I immediately contacted my banks, as well as XXXX, in order to inform them of this fraud. I also called XXXX XXXX, as most of the transfers originated from them. I was eventually able to get in touch with a gentleman named XXXX in their fraud department, who told me that there were several accounts from the same account holder that had been closed due to identity theft, and that an external investigation had already been started. He would give me no further detail. The position that XXXX, Citi, and XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX took was that I needed to work this out with XXXX, and that as far as they were concerned, I owed them the money. This has left my credit score in shambles, caused me extreme stress, and will likely force me into XXXX. I have kept every email, receipt, and letter involved in this matter and offered it to XXXX and they seemed disinterested.
